title: Questions About Political Protection Networks
summary: Drop #20 implied that political figures and institutions protected one another through hidden networks of influence.
raw_text:
Why are certain people protected?
Who provides cover?
Why do investigations stall?
Who benefits from corruption?
Q

lines.fact: Investigations can slow or end for legal, procedural, political, evidentiary, or resource-related reasons.
lines.theory: Q followers viewed stalled investigations as evidence of institutional corruption and protection networks.
lines.context: High-profile investigations often become politically contested and publicly scrutinized.
lines.questions: What evidence distinguishes institutional corruption from ordinary prosecutorial discretion or insufficient evidence?
